model.label: A system of concepts for the continuity of care
model.author: Nicholas Oughtibridge

classes.label: social determinant of health
classes.description: condition in which a subject of care is born, grows, lives, works and ages including their access to power, money and resources

classes.label: appointment
classes.description: arrangement to meet someone at a particular time and place

classes.label: integrated care plan
classes.description: care plan crossing organizational, professional and contextual borders

metadata.value: multi-professional care plan, multi-disciplinary care plan
Integrated care plans are the basis for planning to deliver continuity of care.
Integrated care plans address physical, mental and social needs.

classes.label: care contact
classes.description: interaction between one or more subjects of care and one or more care personnel

dataElements.label: takes place during
dataElements.description: one care contact shall takes place during a contact period

metadata.value: Face to face contact with a general practitioner.
Family visit at the social worker’s office.

classes.label: care personnel
classes.description: person mandated through a care employment

dataElements.label: participates in
dataElements.description: any care personnel shall participates in one or more care contacts

dataElements.label: plays
dataElements.description: any care personnel shall plays one or more care performers

metadata.value: Nurse’s assistant, head of department, medical secretary, receptionist.

classes.label: health record extract
classes.description: part or all of a health record extracted for the purpose of communication

dataElements.label: imported into
dataElements.description: any health record extracts shall imported into one or more data repositories

dataElements.label: extracted from (1)
dataElements.description: health record extracts  extracted from any health records

metadata.value: This document defines one health record extract specialization ‒ the electronic health record extract. However, as the content of a health record is not limited to information in electronic format, the content of health record extracts may be in formats other than electronic.

classes.label: discharge report
classes.description: health report concerning a completed mandated period of care

dataElements.label: concerns
dataElements.description: discharge reports  concerns any mandated periods of care

classes.label: care assessment
classes.description: care activity where an opinion is formed about health conditions of a subject of care

dataElements.label: forms opinion about
dataElements.description: care assessment defines health conditions

classes.label: knowledge
classes.description: maintained, processed, and interpreted information

classes.label: social care report
classes.description: social record extract conveying specifically focused social care information in order to fulfil current information needs of the recipient

metadata.value: Disability level measured adopting a specific scale method.
Report on the home visit done by the social worker.

classes.label: proxy mandate
classes.description: care mandate to take decisions on behalf of a subject of care

metadata.value: A proxy mandate may be explicit, such as a power of attorney, or implicit, such as parental responsibility.
A proxy mandate may be granted through informed consent of the subject of care or acquired by legal means, such as parenthood.

classes.label: care information
classes.description: information about a person relevant to their care

classes.label: social care activity mandate
classes.description: care mandate to provide needed social care

metadata.value: Figure 122 —  social care activity mandate (UML Representation)

classes.label: subject of care preference delay
classes.description: care activity delay by the preference of the subject of care

dataElements.label: caused by
dataElements.description: subject of care preference delays  caused by a subject of care

metadata.value: Surgery delayed enabling the subject of care to undertake seasonal work.
Investigation delayed supporting the subject of care’s scheduling choice.
Figure 97 —  subject of care preference delay (UML Representation)

classes.label: care supporting organization
classes.description: organization that is a care third party

metadata.value: Voluntary aid organization, homecare service organization, health insurance fund, family.

classes.label: care communication
classes.description: care activity where two or more care actors communicate

dataElements.label: takes place between
dataElements.description: care communication takes place between 2 or more care actors

classes.label: self-care performer
classes.description: role played by a subject of care when performing self-care

dataElements.label: delivers
dataElements.description: self-care performers shall delivers one or more self-care

classes.label: request for care
classes.description: request for care provider activities

dataElements.label: triggers
dataElements.description: a request for care shall triggers a care period mandate

dataElements.label: concerns
dataElements.description: requests for care shall concerns one or more care matters

dataElements.label: asks for
dataElements.description: requests for care  asks for any care provider activity

metadata.value: A request for an appointment at a cardiology outpatient department.
An emergency call to a general practitioner for a home visit at night.
A request for care may be expressed either by the subject of care or on their behalf.
A care provider may accept, refer or decline a request for care.
A request for care could result in a care assessment concluding that no other care activities (for example, care investigations or care treatments) should be performed.
A request for the intervention of a community nurse.

classes.label: subject of care desire
classes.description: desire expressed by the subject of care or the subject of care proxy regarding the performance of certain care activities

dataElements.label: considered during
dataElements.description: subject of care desires  considered during any care needs assessments

dataElements.label: recorded in
dataElements.description: subject of care desires  recorded in any health records

dataElements.label: influences
dataElements.description: subject of care desires  influences any care mandates

metadata.value: To specify the place of care (home, professional office, etc...) where to receive a service (psychological support).
Preference for a monetary subsidy instead of a direct social service to support a disabled person.
To prefer home services instead of being moved in a retirement home.
To be supported or treated by same gender social care or care professional.
No blood transfusion, cultural or religious preference, do not resuscitate, allow natural death.
To receive a financial or economic support instead of a social service.
Figure 119 —  subject of care desire (UML Representation)
In quality management [ISO 9000:2015] requirement is defined as “needs and expectations that is stated, generally implied or obligatory”. The expectations reflect the subject of care’s desires.

classes.label: protocol
classes.description: customized care guideline

metadata.value: A protocol is more precise than a care guideline.
A protocol for a heart failure in an emergency department, a protocol for treatment and follow-up of urinary infections in children in a health centre.
Protocols are often presented in a formal manner with respect to the expected behaviours and roles of care actors.

classes.label: health summary
classes.description: electronic health record extract containing essential care information intended for specific uses

metadata.value: epSOS patient summary in the European Union.
Clinical summary in the United States of America.
Continuity of care record in the United States of America.

classes.label: care activity period
classes.description: period during which care activities are performed for a subject of care

dataElements.label: mandated period of care
dataElements.description: mandated period of care may include  care activity period.

classes.label: organization
classes.description: person or group of people that has its own functions with responsibilities, authorities and relationships to achieve its objectives

dataElements.label: is
dataElements.description: a organization shall is one or more care actors

metadata.value: Groupings or subdivisions of organizations may also be considered as organizations where there is need to identify them in this way for purposes of information interchange.
In this document, this definition applies to any kind of organization, whatever their legal status.

classes.label: care period mandate
classes.description: care mandate commissioning a mandated period of care

dataElements.label: commissions
dataElements.description: care period mandates shall commissions one or more mandated periods of care

dataElements.label: authorizes
dataElements.description: care period mandates shall authorizes one or more care provider activity

metadata.value: A care period mandate may be an agreement between the subject of care and a care provider to provide specified care services in a mandated period of care.

classes.label: risk
classes.description: combination of the probability of occurrence of harm and the severity of that harm

metadata.value: The probability of occurrence includes the exposure to a hazardous situation and the possibility to avoid or limit the harm.

classes.label: observed condition
classes.description: health condition observed by a care actor

dataElements.label: observed during
dataElements.description: one observed condition shall observed during one or more health condition periods

metadata.value: Care professionals and subjects of care are examples of care actors that can perceive the observed aspect of a health state.
A blood pressure, a swelling in the abdomen, tachycardia, body weight, lung infiltration on x-ray, a haemoglobin value, pale skin.
An observed condition is a health issue and as such is a representation of aspects of the health state.

classes.label: continuity of care
classes.description: coherent and interconnected series of care events over time

classes.label: care funds
classes.description: resource provided for funding care delivery

dataElements.label: funding
dataElements.description: care funds shall funding one or more care resources

metadata.value: Funds may be provided by, for example, a health insurance fund, a governmental agency, a national or local authority, a welfare programme, the subject of care or any other person or organization having a role in the funding of care.

classes.label: care pathway
classes.description: detailed guidance for each stage in the interdisciplinary management of a subject of care

dataElements.label: addresses
dataElements.description: care pathways shall addresses one or more health issues

dataElements.label: informs
dataElements.description: any care pathways  informs any core care plans

dataElements.label: refers to
dataElements.description: any care pathways  refers to any care guidelines

metadata.value: The concept care pathway includes subtypes, for example, “integrated care pathways”, “multidisciplinary pathways of care collaborative care pathways”.
Care pathways are knowledge supporting the design of core care plans.
Care pathways are designed to support care activity management. They provide detailed guidance for each stage in the management of a subject of care (treatments, interventions, etc.).

classes.label: health concern
classes.description: health record extract that includes all health record components associated with a health thread

metadata.value: A concern is a collection of information supporting continuity of care for a subject of care. A health thread can include care activities, health conditions, care activity planning, activity management, evaluations and assessments. Thereby, all information needed for continuity of care for an individual subject of care can be covered by a health concern.
Health concern is closely related to the “concern tracking” based on HL7 standards.

Health concerns can be constructed to support continuity of care for care processes, continuity of care processes and accumulations or associations of continuity of care processes. This means that concerns can relate to episodes of care contacts, chronic disease management, multiple chronic disease management, etc.
Health concerns can be represented and labelled in electronic health records (EHRs) as extracts or components.

classes.label: personal health record
classes.description: health record held and maintained by the subject of care or a subject of care proxy

classes.label: discharge summary
classes.description: discharge report summarizing a mandated period of care

metadata.value: One mandated period of care may be immediately followed by another; the next mandated care provider will then be a main recipient of the discharge summary.
A discharge summary may be provided to the subject of care.

Discharge summaries are often meant to be processed so as to categorize hospital stays according to a statistically designed classification (such as the Diagnosis-Related Groups (DRGs) or other systems), usually for funding or epidemiological purposes.
“Uniform Hospital Discharge Data Set” and “Uniform Ambulatory Medical Care Minimum Data Set” in the USA.
“Résumé de sortie standardisé” (RSS) or “Résumé d'Unité Médicale” (RUM) in France.

classes.label: care provider
classes.description: care actor that is able to be assigned one or more care period mandates

dataElements.label: causes
dataElements.description: any care providers shall causes one or more resource delays

dataElements.label: manages
dataElements.description: any care providers shall manages one or more episode of cares

dataElements.label: states
dataElements.description: any care providers shall states one or more care commitments

dataElements.label: has
dataElements.description: care providers shall has one or more care service directories

dataElements.label: responsible for
dataElements.description: care providers  has care period mandate for any mandated periods of care

dataElements.label: delivers
dataElements.description: any care providers shall delivers one or more care provider activity

dataElements.label: able to be assigned
dataElements.description: any care providers shall able to be assigned one or more care period mandates

dataElements.label: accepts or declines
dataElements.description: any care providers shall accepts or declines one or more requests for care

dataElements.label: offers
dataElements.description: care providers shall offers one or more care service

dataElements.label: receives
dataElements.description: any care providers shall receives one or more requests for care

dataElements.label: arranges
dataElements.description: any care providers shall arranges one or more initial contacts

metadata.value: According to this definition, organizations solely responsible for the funding, payment, or reimbursement of care provision are not care providers; for the purpose of this document, they are considered as care third parties.
The personnel of a care organization that is a care provider may be care professionals and other care personnel who participate in the provision of care.

This document includes only two specializations of care provider. This is not meant to exclude the possibility of other specializations. In jurisdictions where other kinds of care actors are included in the concept of care provider, the necessary specializations may be added.

classes.label: vulnerability
classes.description: health condition that is an exposure to the risk of harm

dataElements.label: results in
dataElements.description: vulnerabilities shall results in one or more risk conditions

metadata.value: Immunosuppressed leading to risk of infection.

In the perspective of healthcare and wellbeing, the term “vulnerable condition” is often used to label frail citizens or socio-economic and education conditions (e.g. housing, job, disability), or both, that motivate certain interventions in social care, generally addressing a target population (e.g. older persons, children, families, homeless, refugees).
Lives up a steep flight of stairs leading to increased risk of falls.
The potential harm is a risk condition.

classes.label: self-care
classes.description: care activity performed by the subject of care

dataElements.label: generates
dataElements.description: self-care  generates any non-ratified care information

metadata.value: Self injection of insulin, self-measurement of blood pressure, or of glycaemia, yoga, taking a bath, meditation, mindfulness.

classes.label: care plan
classes.description: dynamic, personalized plan including identified needed care activity, intended outcomes and care goals, relating to one or more specified health needs in a care process

dataElements.label: recorded in
dataElements.description: care plan shall recorded in one or more health records

dataElements.label: based upon
dataElements.description: any care plan shall based upon one or more core care plans

dataElements.label: conforms to
dataElements.description: any care plan shall conforms to one or more care guidelines

dataElements.label: addresses (1)
dataElements.description: care plan shall addresses one or more health threads

dataElements.label: result of
dataElements.description: care plan shall result of one or more care planning

dataElements.label: complies with
dataElements.description: any care plan may include any care plan.

metadata.value: A care plan can be subdivided from different perspectives by different constraints.
Plan for social care activities to be performed by one care provider within a social care context.
A social worker care plan with the constraint of only one specific social care provider involved.

classes.label: clinical issue
classes.description: health issue that is a clinical matter

classes.label: third party care performer
classes.description: role performed by a care third party

dataElements.label: delivers
dataElements.description: third party care performers shall delivers one or more care third party activities

metadata.value: Figure 43 —  third party care performer (UML Representation)

classes.label: health thread
classes.description: defined association between health matters as determined by one or more care actors

dataElements.label: links
dataElements.description: any health threads shall links one or more health threads

dataElements.label: delineates
dataElements.description: a health thread shall delineates one or more episodes of care bundles

metadata.value: Under the responsibility of a designated care actor, a health thread linking several health matters can describe an episodes of care bundle, for instance a partial or comprehensive synthesis of care actor-related episodes of care.

A low back pain, known for many years by the subject of care’s general practitioner (GP), treated several times by the physiotherapist who labelled it a scoliosis and currently a candidate for a specific orthopaedic intervention.
Type 2 diabetes treated by a GP, a nurse, an endocrinologist and a vascular surgeon.
A health thread may be established by a team (e.g. a coordination committee).
A health thread can be built step-by-step, by allowing each care professional to add their perspective into a common health thread.
A health thread may be considered an aggregation of health matters or health threads, or both.
A collective decision (before, during or after the care interventions) may define a health thread and so the idea of the “episode” accepted by all the care professionals involved.
The health conditions included in a care process.
A health thread reconciles a range of health matters reflecting the variety of scopes of care actors, particularly of care providers.

classes.label: core care plan
classes.description: knowledge providing reusable content addressing one or more specified health issues

metadata.value: A core care plan may include advanced formulated schemes for recommended care activities.
Core care plans may be applied in care planning as a continuity of care process management method.
A core care plan is usually based upon knowledge in care guidelines (including protocols).

classes.label: health record component
classes.description: part of a health record that is identifiable for the purposes of referencing and revision

metadata.value: This document defines one health record component specialization, the electronic record component. However, as the content of a health record is not limited to information in electronic format, the content of health record components may be in formats other than electronic.
A health record component may itself result from an aggregation of multiple health record components.

classes.label: health certificate
classes.description: official document issued by a care actor attesting to the relevant health matters of a subject of care

dataElements.label: attests
dataElements.description: health certificates  attests any care matters

dataElements.label: issued by
dataElements.description: any health certificates shall issued by one or more care actors

metadata.value: certificate related to a health matter

classes.label: risk condition
classes.description: health condition representing a potential undesired future health state

metadata.value: A risk condition can be a result of a current vulnerability.
While a risk is defined as the combination of a probability of an event and its consequences, the risk condition deals only with the consequences.

classes.label: medical device
classes.description:
instrument, apparatus, implement, machine, appliance, implant, reagent for in vitro use, software, material or other similar or related article, intended by the manufacturer to be used, alone or in combination, for human beings, for one or more of the specific medical purposes of
— diagnosis, prevention, monitoring, treatment or alleviation of disease,
— diagnosis, monitoring, treatment, alleviation of or compensation for an injury,
— investigation, replacement, modification, or support of the anatomy or of a physiological process,
— supporting or sustaining life,
— control of conception,
— disinfection of medical devices,
— providing information by means of in vitro examination of specimens derived from the human body,
and which does not achieve its primary intended action by pharmacological, immunological or metabolic means, in or on the human body, but which can be assisted in its intended function by such means

classes.label: care
classes.description: provision of accommodations, comfort and treatment to an individual subject of care

classes.label: social matter
classes.description: health matter concerning how a subject of care lives in their social environment

classes.label: prognostic condition
classes.description: potential health condition representing the expected course of a health state as assessed by care professionals

dataElements.label: based on
dataElements.description: prognostic conditions  based on any professionally assessed conditions

classes.label: care treatment
classes.description: care activity intended to directly improve or maintain a health state

dataElements.label: influences
dataElements.description: one care treatment  influences any health states

metadata.value: Care treatment includes all types of activities that intend to influence the health state including, for example, rehabilitation, palliative care, education of subjects of care and carers, etc.
The treatment of a subject of care’s health state based on that subject of care’s health conditions, is a part of the continuity of care process considered from a therapeutic perspective.
Care treatment is intended to contribute to fulfilling the assessed health need.
Prevention is a type of care treatment where the risk of a health condition is treated.

classes.label: event
classes.description: situation considered to occur at a time point

classes.label: other carer
classes.description: person being a care third party

classes.label: episodes of care bundle
classes.description: group of episodes of care delineated by a health thread

metadata.value: An episodes of care bundle starts with the very contact with a care provider for a health issue considered in a health thread and ends after the completion of all care activities related to the last contact with any care provider for a health issue encompassed in the same health thread.
From the electronic health record point of view, an episodes of care bundle shows the overall care activity period elements related to those health matters that are linked by the same health thread.

As different health threads may be considered that reconcile the perspectives of different care actors (e.g. a care team manager, or a health authority) or sets of care actors, there can exist different health threads according to the specific perspectives of those sets of care actors that justify building up such health threads. Therefore, there can exist as many episodes of care bundles as there are such health threads.
A group of episodes addressing diabetes mellitus with ocular and renal complications.
Figure 93 —  episodes of care bundle (UML Representation)

classes.label: automatic medical device
classes.description: medical device capable of performing care activities by itself or under human supervision

dataElements.label: delivers
dataElements.description: any automatic medical devices shall delivers one or more automated care

classes.label: prescribed self-care
classes.description: self-care prescribed by a care professional

dataElements.label: takes place during
dataElements.description: prescribed self-care shall takes place during a prescribed self-care period

classes.label: summarized care information repository
classes.description: data repository containing summarized information for care coordination and the continuity of care

metadata.value: Figure 144 —  summarized care information repository (UML Representation)

classes.label: health need
classes.description: health matter causing a deficit in the current health state compared to aspects of a desired future health state

dataElements.label: deficit in
dataElements.description: one health need shall deficit in a health state

dataElements.label: background for
dataElements.description: any health needs shall background for one or more reasons for request for care

dataElements.label: considered during
dataElements.description: any health needs shall considered during one or more care needs assessments

metadata.value: The health need can be identified and formulated by the subject of care or by any other care actor.
Health needs indicate and motivate care activities. They are the basic input to care needs assessments.
The desired future health state can be an intended outcome expressed as target condition.

classes.label: health problem list
classes.description: health thread linking a set of health problems

classes.label: care guideline
classes.description: set of systematically developed statements based on knowledge to assist the decisions made by care actors about care activities to be performed with regard to specified health issues

dataElements.label: centred on
dataElements.description: care guidelines shall centred on one or more health issues

metadata.value: Care guidelines are ordinarily structured and contain standard criteria and indicators for measurement.
Care guidelines related to hypertension, diabetes, pregnancy follow-up, Caesarean section, pressure area management.

Care guidelines are a knowledge resource.  They are generic and concern no actual subject of care in particular. They generally reflect a broad statement of good practice and may include multiple operational details.
Social intervention programmes for people with a learning disability.

classes.label: folder
classes.description: record component used for creating hierarchical structures within an electronic health record extract and with compositions as leaf nodes

dataElements.label: folder
dataElements.description: any folders  folder any folders

classes.label: care third party
classes.description: care actor other than a care provider or the subject of care

dataElements.label: plays
dataElements.description: any care third parties shall plays one or more third party care performers

dataElements.label: supports
dataElements.description: care third parties shall supports one or more subjects of care

metadata.value: Care third party is an abstract concept. It is fully represented by one or more specific subordinate parties.

classes.label: authorization by law
classes.description: provision in legislation that, in certain circumstances, may overrule the need for informed consent

metadata.value: A care mandate requires either informed consent or authorization by law.

classes.label: care professional
classes.description: person holding a care professional entitlement

dataElements.label: has
dataElements.description: care professionals  has any care professional entitlements

dataElements.label: maintains
dataElements.description: any care professionals shall maintains one or more professional health records

dataElements.label: manages demand
dataElements.description: any care professionals shall manages demand one or more requests for care

dataElements.label: manages
dataElements.description: any care professionals shall manages one or more care activity periods

dataElements.label: ratifies
dataElements.description: any care professionals shall ratifies one or more care information for import

dataElements.label: issues (1)
dataElements.description: any care professionals shall issues one or more referrals

dataElements.label: plays
dataElements.description: a care professional shall plays one or more prescribers

dataElements.label: considers
dataElements.description: any care professionals considers one or more considered conditions

dataElements.label: determines
dataElements.description: any care professionals shall determines one or more working diagnoses

dataElements.label: rules out
dataElements.description: any care professionals shall rules out one or more excluded conditions

dataElements.label: performs
dataElements.description: any care professionals shall performs one or more care needs assessments

dataElements.label: identifies (1)
dataElements.description: any care professionals shall identifies one or more prognostic conditions

metadata.value: General practitioner, medical consultant, therapist, dentist, nurse, radiographer, social worker.
The care professional entitlement entitles a care professional to provide care independent of a role in a care organization.

classes.label: needed care activity
classes.description: care activity bundle which includes those care activities assessed as needed to address a specified health need

dataElements.label: addresses
dataElements.description: needed care activities shall addresses one or more health needs

metadata.value: Needed care activities are managed in a care plan.
Needed care activities are the outcome of care needs assessments performed by mandated care professionals for a subject of care.

classes.label: resource
classes.description: asset that is utilized or consumed during the execution of activities

metadata.value: Time, personnel, human skills and knowledge, equipment, services, supplies, facilities, technology, data, money.
Resources may be reusable, renewable or consumable.
Utilities such as power, water, fuel and communication infrastructures.
Resources are used, consumed or renewed during activities as part of a process.

classes.label: social care plan
classes.description: care plan addressing social issues

classes.label: health problem
classes.description: health issue considered by a care actor to be a problem

metadata.value: Health problems can be single observations but are usually more compound as a summary of several observations. Single observations are often criteria for the more compound health condition considered to be a health problem.

classes.label: prescriber
classes.description: role performed by a care professional when recommending or authorising a care activity

dataElements.label: prescribes (1)
dataElements.description: any prescribers shall prescribes a prescribed self-care

classes.label: encounter
classes.description: care activity period during which a contact occurs

metadata.value: Since during a contact more than one health issue may be addressed, it may relate to more than one care process and more than one episode of care.

classes.label: care data
classes.description: data produced during care activities

classes.label: shareable data repository
classes.description: data repository containing health record extracts, accessible for duly authorized care actors independent of their organizational affiliation and placed under the custody of a care actor

metadata.value: A shareable data repository shall be placed under the custody of a care actor in order to assure and maintain its consistency.

classes.label: clinical care activity mandate
classes.description: care mandate to provide needed care activities concerned with the physical or mental health of a subject of care

metadata.value: Figure 121 —  clinical care activity mandate (UML Representation)

classes.label: health issue
classes.description: health condition motivating care

metadata.value: Loss of weight, heart attack, drug addiction, injury, dermatitis, requirement for a health certificate, inability to prepare meals, suitability for immunisation

classes.label: care third party activity
classes.description: care activity performed by a care third party

dataElements.label: generates
dataElements.description: any care third party activities  generates any non-ratified care information

dataElements.label: requires
dataElements.description: care third party activities  requires any care activity mandates

metadata.value: Care treatment on advice by a chemist.
The care treatment of a bedsore by a subject of care’s relation.
Figure 58 —  care third party activity (UML Representation)

classes.label: care information request
classes.description: request sent out by a care actor to another care actor for specific care information needed for the provision of care to a subject of care

dataElements.label: topic for
dataElements.description: care information requests  topic for any health record extracts

metadata.value: In order to fulfil the request, a mandate to export personal information is needed.

classes.label: care employment
classes.description: mandate given by a care organization to a care personnel describing the roles and responsibilities assigned to that care personnel

dataElements.label: mandates
dataElements.description: care employments mandate care personnel

classes.label: resultant condition
classes.description: health condition representing an output health state

metadata.value: Care process result, care activity result.

A resultant condition can represent the output health state after a single care activity, a bundle of care investigations or care treatments, or both, in a care process, or the outcome after a complete continuity of care process.

classes.label: output health state
classes.description: health state at the end of a care process

dataElements.label: observed as
dataElements.description: any output health states shall observed as one or more resultant conditions

classes.label: continuity of care process
classes.description: care process, encompassing all care provider activities and other prescribed care activities, that addresses identified or specified health issues

dataElements.label: addresses
dataElements.description: continuity of care processes shall addresses one or more health issues

dataElements.label: includes
dataElements.description: continuity of care processes shall includes one or more mandated periods of care

metadata.value: In a continuity of care process a subject of care and care professionals interact in all types of care activities.
A continuity of care process comprises all kinds of care activities, including self-care activities as prescribed or recommended by care professionals.
The continuity of care process can be regarded as the process to support continuity of care from the perspective of the subject of care.
Figure 73 —  continuity of care process (UML Representation)

classes.label: subject of care proxy
classes.description: care actor exercising a proxy mandate

dataElements.label: exercises
dataElements.description: one subject of care proxy  exercises any proxy mandates

dataElements.label: maintains
dataElements.description: any subject of care proxy  maintains any personal health record

dataElements.label: acts for
dataElements.description: one subject of care proxy  acts for any subjects of care

dataElements.label: has
dataElements.description: any subject of care proxy  has a consent competence

dataElements.label: expresses
dataElements.description: any subject of care proxy  expresses any subject of care desires

dataElements.label: states
dataElements.description: a subject of care proxy  states any objections

dataElements.label: gives
dataElements.description: any subject of care proxy  gives any informed consents

metadata.value: Figure 40 —  subject of care proxy (UML Representation)

classes.label: record template
classes.description: information model that is a template for recording a care record

metadata.value: care record form; form design; openEHR template; FHIR questionnaire
A care record template acts as a data constraint on a health record used in specific care provision use cases.
In HL7 FHIR, care record templates are known as questionnaires; the completed health record is a questionnaire response.

ISO/IEC 19763-13 provides a metamodel for form design registration suitable for representing care record templates; care record templates are known as form designs in both ISO/IEC 19763-13 and ISO 215261).
In openEHR, record templates are known as templates.

classes.label: composition
classes.description: health record component containing information composed during one clinical encounter or documentation session, and committed within one health record

metadata.value: A composition represents the set of record components composed (authored) during one clinical encounter or documentation session and committed within one health record.

classes.label: mandate to export personal information
classes.description: care mandate implying the right to communicate health record extracts

dataElements.label: implies the right to communicate
dataElements.description: mandates to export personal information shall implies the right to communicate one or more health record extracts

dataElements.label: concerns
dataElements.description: any mandates to export personal information  concerns any care matters

metadata.value: A request to a general practitioner by a subject of care for the transfer of their health record to another general practitioner, when that subject of care moves to another part of the country.
A request to a doctor to write a letter to a medical specialist stating the reasons of a referral.
Figure 127 —  mandate to export personal information (UML Representation)

classes.label: automated care
classes.description: care initiated by a care actor and thereafter delivered by an automatic medical device

dataElements.label: results in
dataElements.description: any automated care  results in any non-ratified care information

dataElements.label: initiated during
dataElements.description: one automated care  initiated during any care activities

dataElements.label: terminated during
dataElements.description: one automated care  terminated during any care activities

dataElements.label: reviewed during
dataElements.description: automated care  reviewed during any care activities

metadata.value: Automated care itself is not a care activity because a device cannot hold responsibility. The care actor who initiates and reviews automated care remains responsible for the safe use of the automatic medical device.
Activities performed by the machine during a long-term Electrocardiography (“Holter recording”) programme, implanted cardiac defibrillator.

classes.label: continuity facilitator mandate
classes.description: care mandate assigning the right and obligation to monitor and coordinate the delivery of care to one subject of care including the right to retrieve relevant information and obligation to document all activities based on this mandate

dataElements.label: applies to
dataElements.description: continuity facilitator mandates  applies to any care period mandates

dataElements.label: concerns
dataElements.description: continuity facilitator mandates shall concerns one or more health threads

metadata.value: Beyond solely assuming the function described above, a continuity facilitator may also assume the function of a lead or coordinator (or both) of care activities delivered to the subject of care.
A mandate assigned to a coordinating nurse, to a lead general practitioner, keyworker, etc. to coordinate care.
For continuity of care, the continuity facilitator mandates for complete continuity of care processes are of special importance from the subject of care’s perspective.
A continuity facilitation can be fulfilled only if the involved care actors have the information needed to perform their tasks in care activities.

classes.label: care evaluation
classes.description: care activity where aspects of at least one other care activity are evaluated

dataElements.label: evaluates aspects of
dataElements.description: care evaluations  evaluates aspects of any care activities

metadata.value: Care evaluation may be performed by any care actor, including the subject of care and relevant third parties.

classes.label: potential health condition
classes.description: possible future or current health condition described by a care actor

metadata.value: A potential health condition is not yet observed, but represents an imagined, possible observation of aspects of a current or future health state.
A potential health condition can only be fully supported using one of its specializations.

classes.label: health state
classes.description: overall level of health of a subject of care

dataElements.label: concerns
dataElements.description: one health state shall concerns a person

metadata.value: The underlying health state is present, even if not observed.  For example, the subject of care having a cancer before it gives symptoms.

classes.label: entry
classes.description: health record component that represents the data documenting one  observation or a series of related observations, or a  statement, an explanation of reasoning, a care intention, plan or action

dataElements.label: composition
dataElements.description: composition may include  entry.

classes.label: referral
classes.description: request for care where a care professional asks a care provider to make a care commitment for a care period mandate

dataElements.label: initiates
dataElements.description: referrals shall initiates a care contact

dataElements.label: asks for
dataElements.description: one referral shall asks for a care commitment

metadata.value: An accepted referral transfers the continuity responsibility for the health issues specified in the referral.
A referral from an orthopaedic surgeon to a rehabilitation service.

classes.label: health condition delay
classes.description: care activity delay caused by a health condition

metadata.value: Vaccination delayed because the subject of care has a contraindicating infection.
Surgery delayed because the subject of care is pregnant.

classes.label: objection
classes.description: subject of care desire that specific care activities are not performed

dataElements.label: requires
dataElements.description: objections shall requires a consent competence

dataElements.label: limits
dataElements.description: any objections shall limits one or more care mandates

classes.label: adverse event
classes.description: unintended event that has negative impact upon a care process

dataElements.label: requires
dataElements.description: adverse events shall requires a adverse event management

dataElements.label: negatively influences
dataElements.description: adverse events shall negatively influences one or more care processes

metadata.value: Adverse events can occur during appropriate care activities.
A mishap during correctly performed care activity.
A drug can be used as recommended but cause allergic reaction when the drug allergy was not known before the treatment.
'Iatrogenesis' or 'iatrogenic' is a common cause of adverse events.
An accident in connection to a care activity.
An infection acquired during a hospital stay.

classes.label: facility
classes.description: place, building or equipment used for a particular purpose

classes.label: episode of care
classes.description: health related period during which care activities are performed to address one health issue as identified by one care professional

dataElements.label: episodes of care bundle
dataElements.description: any episodes of care bundle may include  episode of care.

dataElements.label: centred on
dataElements.description: episode of cares shall centred on one or more health issues

metadata.value: An episode of care encompasses all care activities related to the same health issue.
During a mandated period of care several health issues may be handled and as such be linked to several episodes of care. These episodes of care are said to be “concurrent”.
Period where a urinary tract infection is managed.
Period where a cholecystectomy is performed.

For practical reasons (e.g. the need to state start and end dates) and also because it relates specifically to a health issue defined by a given care professional, an episode of care does not necessarily coincide with an “episode of illness” (or an episode of disease), or an episode of any other kind of health issue.

An episode of care starts with the very initial contact with a care provider for a health matter and it ends after the completion of all care activity periods related to the last contact with that care provider for the same health matter.

classes.label: process model
classes.description: representation of a process

classes.label: intended outcome
classes.description: desired achievement of a care process addressing health needs

dataElements.label: addresses
dataElements.description: intended outcomes  addresses any health needs

metadata.value: To increase the survival of a subject of care with breast cancer in a breast cancer screening programme.
To decrease a subject of care risk of secondary effects to diabetes.
An intended outcome can be expressed as one or several target conditions to be reached within a specified date and time.
To reduce a subject of care risk of morbidity and mortality from tobacco-related diseases in a smoking cessation programme.

classes.label: care activity
classes.description: activity intended to improve or maintain a health state

dataElements.label: care process
dataElements.description: a number of care process shall be a part of  care activity.

dataElements.label: requires
dataElements.description: care activities  requires any care resources

dataElements.label: for
dataElements.description: any care activities shall for a subject of care

dataElements.label: recorded in
dataElements.description: care activities shall recorded in one or more health records

dataElements.label: targets
dataElements.description: care activities shall targets one or more care goals

dataElements.label: care activity
dataElements.description: care activity may include  care activity.

dataElements.label: performed during
dataElements.description: care activities shall performed during one or more care activity periods

dataElements.label: care
dataElements.description: a number of care shall be a part of  care activity.

metadata.value: In this document, only activities concerning continuity of care are included. Consequently, in this document, all care activities are part of processes under clinical governance.
A blood pressure measurement completed by a care professional including component care activities of taking, documenting and evaluation.
A care activity maintaining health state includes activities confirming a health state, e.g. a medical statement to an insurance company or the driving licence authority.

classes.label: unintended event
classes.description: phenomenon that is not part of the normal course of a process

metadata.value: Activities in a process are deliberate and have a purpose. In an ideal situation purposes are always fulfilled. If an activity in whatever other process has an impact on the process currently analysed, the effect of this activity is perceived by the current process as an unintended event. Then the course of the process may deviate from the expected one. Such an exception from the desired course might prove negative or positive in comparison to the desired process outcome.
Spontaneous recovery when the subject of care is awaiting therapy.
Surgical complication (anatomy and tissue reacts in an unexpected manner).
An unintended event can be either expected or unexpected.

classes.label: target condition
classes.description: potential health condition representing either intended outcomes or care goals

dataElements.label: represents
dataElements.description: target conditions shall represents one or more intended outcomes

metadata.value: The target condition of a newly diagnosed diabetic adolescent boy is maintenance of his HbA1c at less than 48 mmol/mol. [HbA1c is a lab test that shows the average level of blood sugar (glucose) over the previous 3 months; it shows how well diabetes is being controlled].
Assessment of needs for care activities includes identification of intended outcomes or care goals. These inform decisions about relevant activities to create or update the care plan.
The target condition for a worker that arrived at the emergency room with a broken arm is to be fully functional for work in the shortest period.

classes.label: excluded condition
classes.description: considered condition that one or more care professionals have determined not to be consistent with the known observed conditions

dataElements.label: not consistent with
dataElements.description: excluded conditions  not consistent with any observed conditions

metadata.value: discounted condition, non-verified condition, ruled out condition, ruled out considered condition

classes.label: subject of care
classes.description: person seeking to receive, receiving, or having received care

dataElements.label: has or has had
dataElements.description: any subjects of care shall has or has had a health need

dataElements.label: seeks to receive, is receiving, or has received
dataElements.description: subjects of care shall seeks to receive, is receiving, or has received one or more care

dataElements.label: states
dataElements.description: any subjects of care  states a objection

dataElements.label: maintains
dataElements.description: any subjects of care shall maintains one or more personal health record

dataElements.label: participates in
dataElements.description: any subjects of care shall participates in one or more care contacts

dataElements.label: has (1)
dataElements.description: any subjects of care  has a consent competence

dataElements.label: expresses (1)
dataElements.description: any subjects of care  expresses a subject of care desire

dataElements.label: gives
dataElements.description: any subjects of care  gives a informed consent

dataElements.label: plays
dataElements.description: a subject of care shall plays a self-care performer

metadata.value: Each member of a group of diabetic people attending a session of medical education.
Each member of a target population for screening.
A fœtus may be considered as a subject of care when receiving care.

classes.label: data
classes.description: set of values of qualitative or quantitative variables

classes.label: resource delay
classes.description: care activity delay caused by resource constraints

metadata.value: Care activity postponed while necessary financial resources are identified.
Care activity scheduled later than clinically indicated to allow resources to be deployed on other subjects of care (a waiting list).

classes.label: care goal
classes.description: desired achievement of one or more care activities

dataElements.label: contributes to achievement of
dataElements.description: care goals  contributes to achievement of any intended outcomes

metadata.value: To increase the subject of care's adherence to the treatment in a hypertension programme.
To increase the subject of care’s knowledge of their disease through an educational programme for diabetics.
A set of care goals may constitute an intended outcome.
To stabilise the subject of care’s systolic and diastolic pressure in a hypertension programme.
A care goal shall be expressed as one or several target conditions to be reached within a specified date and time.

classes.label: reason for request for care
classes.description: motive for a request for care

dataElements.label: motivates
dataElements.description: reasons for request for care shall motivates one or more requests for care

metadata.value: There are needs for both direct care (care investigations and care treatments) and indirect care (care assessments and care planning).
Figure 80 —  reason for request for care (UML Representation)

classes.label: prescribed self-care period
classes.description: care activity period where prescribed self-care activity is performed

classes.label: input health state
classes.description: health state at the start of a care process

dataElements.label: input to
dataElements.description: a input health state shall input to a care process

metadata.value: The output health state from one care process may be the input health state to a subsequent care process.

classes.label: care professional entitlement
classes.description: professional care mandate held by a person concerning specific roles in care

metadata.value: Entitlement is usually backed by evidence of having received, or continuously receiving, the necessary qualifications, the relevant education and training.
The official entitlement of a care professional forms the foundation for their official duties and rights.

classes.label: care process
classes.description: set of interrelated or interacting care activities which transforms inputs into outputs

dataElements.label: care process
dataElements.description: care process may include any care process.

dataElements.label: outputs
dataElements.description: one care process shall outputs one or more output health states

dataElements.label: documented in
dataElements.description: care processes shall documented in one or more health records

dataElements.label: planned in
dataElements.description: care processes shall planned in one or more care plan

dataElements.label: performed for
dataElements.description: care processes shall performed for a subject of care

dataElements.label: delivers
dataElements.description: results in

metadata.value: A care process is not by definition restricted to one care provider or any other organizational unit borders.

classes.label: mandated period of care
classes.description: set of care activity periods where a care provider is mandated to perform the care activities required to address specific health needs

dataElements.label: documented in
dataElements.description: mandated periods of care  documented in any professional health records

metadata.value: A series of radiotherapy sessions at an outpatient clinic.

Whenever the care provider considered in a mandated period of care is a care organization this mandated period of care encompasses all care activity periods with care professionals who have a role in that care organization.
The relevant information describing and produced during the care activities performed as a part of a mandated period of care is recorded in one or more professional health records.
A mandated period of care may be part of another mandated period of care.

The mandated period of care is focused upon the framework of a care commitment of the provider as well as the mandate from the subject of care which means that the roles and responsibilities of both the interacting parts are respected.
Figure 85 —  mandated period of care (UML Representation)

classes.label: social care
classes.description: care activity supporting a subject of care to live in their social environment

classes.label: care organization
classes.description: organization being a care provider

dataElements.label: adopts
dataElements.description: any care organizations shall adopts one or more core care plans

metadata.value: Groupings or subdivisions of an organization, such as departments or sub-departments, may also be considered as organizations when there is a need to identify them. The internal structure of an organization is described by its organizational pattern. Therefore, an organization may be considered as a standalone organization or as a superstructure containing departments and sub-departments, for instance, other lower-level organizations. A care organization represents the role any such organization plays when it is involved in the direct provision of care activities.
A hospital, a hospital department, or a hospital care unit.
A non-governmental organization or a voluntary association providing care (including social care).
A social department or a municipality providing care (including social care).
A group practice or a self-employed general practitioner.

classes.label: consent competence
classes.description: capability of the subject of care or the subject of care proxy, or both, to give or withhold informed consent

classes.label: information
classes.description: meaningful data

dataElements.label: shared during
dataElements.description: any information  shared during any continuity of care

metadata.value: Information is something that is meaningful. Data might be regarded as information once its meaning is revealed.
Facts, events, things, processes, and ideas, including concepts, are examples of objects.

classes.label: care service
classes.description: result of a care process

classes.label: care actor
classes.description: organization or person participating in care

dataElements.label: assigns
dataElements.description: any care actors shall assigns one or more care mandates

dataElements.label: performs
dataElements.description: any care actors shall performs one or more care activities

dataElements.label: makes decisions assisted by
dataElements.description: any care actors shall makes decisions assisted by one or more care guidelines

dataElements.label: provides
dataElements.description: any care actors shall provides one or more care funds

dataElements.label: identifies
dataElements.description: any care actors shall identifies one or more care matters

dataElements.label: expresses
dataElements.description: any care actors shall expresses one or more requests for care

dataElements.label: sends out
dataElements.description: any care actors shall sends out one or more care information requests

dataElements.label: observes
dataElements.description: any care actors shall observes one or more observed conditions

dataElements.label: responsible for
dataElements.description: a care actor shall responsible for one or more automated care

dataElements.label: defines
dataElements.description: any care actors shall defines one or more health threads

dataElements.label: applies
dataElements.description: any care actors shall applies one or more care plan

dataElements.label: describes
dataElements.description: any care actors shall describes one or more potential health conditions

dataElements.label: custodian for
dataElements.description: one care actor shall custodian for one or more sharable data repositories

dataElements.label: receives (1)
dataElements.description: any care actors shall receives one or more care information requests

metadata.value: The involvement of the care actor can either be direct (for example, the actual provision of care) or indirect (for example, at organizational level).

According to this definition, people or organizations responsible for the funding, payment, or reimbursement of care provision are care actors, as are people and organizations responsible for care delivery.  If a person or organization only funds care and does not deliver care, it is a care actor but not a care provider.

classes.label: care provider activity
classes.description: care activity performed by a care provider

dataElements.label: requires
dataElements.description: care provider activity shall requires one or more care activity mandates

dataElements.label: addresses
dataElements.description: care provider activity shall addresses one or more health issues

dataElements.label: generates
dataElements.description: any care provider activity shall generates one or more care information for import

metadata.value: A care provider activity can be performed in relation to several care activity period elements of the same care activity period.

classes.label: professional health record
classes.description: health record held under the responsibility of one care provider and maintained by care professionals

metadata.value: Health records held at their surgery or at a health centre by a general practitioner, by a medical specialist, by a nurse, in a hospital department at a patient’s bedside, by a care team in an integrated clinical network, by a dentist.
The responsible care provider may allow the subject of care to access or offer contributions to the professional health record.

classes.label: request mandate
classes.description: care mandate implying the right and obligation to request care activities

dataElements.label: permits
dataElements.description: any request mandate shall permits one or more mandates to export personal information

dataElements.label: gives way to
dataElements.description: any request mandate shall gives way to one or more care period mandates

metadata.value: Every care professional with authority (mandate to manage health matters of a subject of care) is entitled to request services and has a request mandate.

A request for care is usually made by a subject of care themselves, but there are circumstances where the subject of care is not in the position of making a request for care. In that case, it may be made on their behalf by another person, such as decision-making representative with power of attorney.
A passer-by may, in some jurisdictions, be obliged to seek care for any endangered person, for example unconscious after a road accident.

Within certain jurisdictions a generic request mandate may be explicitly or implicitly assigned to certain categories of citizens on the basis of their specific roles. It may also be directly or indirectly made necessary by law that such generic mandates are explicitly established.
A subject of care requesting care from a general practitioner.
Care for a child requested by a parent.

classes.label: party
classes.description: person or group performing a role in relation to the business of a specific community or domain

classes.label: professionally assessed condition
classes.description: observed condition assessed by a care professional

classes.label: social environment
classes.description: immediate physical and social setting in which a person lives and interacts

classes.label: care needs assessment
classes.description: care assessment during which a care professional considers a subject of care’shealth needs and determines needed care activities

dataElements.label: informs
dataElements.description: any care needs assessments shall informs one or more care planning

dataElements.label: determines
dataElements.description: any care needs assessments shall determine one or more needed care activities

metadata.value: Care needs assessments should be performed in a dialogue with the subject of care. The responsibility for a care needs assessment is held by a care professional.
Subjects of care interact with care professionals and describe their views and preferences about which care activities to request.
A care needs assessment precedes care planning.

classes.label: care activity mandate
classes.description: care mandate assigning the right and obligation to perform specific care activities

classes.label: care documenting
classes.description: care activity where health records are created or maintained

dataElements.label: maintains
dataElements.description: care documenting shall maintains one or more health records

classes.label: health record
classes.description: data repository regarding the health and care of a subject of care

dataElements.label: accessed during
dataElements.description: any health records  accessed during any care activities

dataElements.label: concerns
dataElements.description: health records shall concerns one or more subjects of care

metadata.value: A health record may include, for example, medical records, dental records, social care provider activity records.

classes.label: clinical need
classes.description: health need related to the body or mind

classes.label: data repository
classes.description: identifiable data storage facility

classes.label: medicinal product
classes.description: pharmaceutical product or combination of pharmaceutical products that may be administered to human beings for treating or preventing disease, with the aim or purpose of making a medical diagnosis or to restore, correct or modify physiological functions

metadata.value: A medicinal product may contain in the packaging one or more manufactured items and one or more pharmaceutical products. In certain regions, a medicinal product may also be defined as any substance or combination of substances which may be used to make a medical diagnosis.

classes.label: initial request for care
classes.description: first request for care concerning one or more specific health matters to be assessed by a care provider

dataElements.label: results in
dataElements.description: one initial request for care shall results in a initial contact

metadata.value: Figure 77 —  initial request for care (UML Representation)

classes.label: health condition
classes.description: aspect of a health state

classes.label: clinical matter
classes.description: health matter concerned with the body or mind

classes.label: health approach
classes.description: episode of care during which the care activities performed address one specific care goal

dataElements.label: addresses
dataElements.description: health approaches shall addresses a care goal

metadata.value: care approach, goal-addressing episode of care

During an episode of care, social activities can include family therapy, medical treatment of subject of care abusing their partner, and supporting talk therapy for the partner. A health approach can be restricted to talk therapy for the partner (with the goal to make them calmer).

During an episode of care a subject of care with diabetes mellitus is monitored and prescribed medication, self-control of habits of living. A health approach can mean that the blood glucose value shall be kept between 5 mmol/l and 8 mmol/l.
While the episode of care focuses on the health issue addressed by the activities, the health approach focuses on the desired outcome of these activities.

classes.label: care process evaluation
classes.description: care evaluation where a care process is evaluated against requirements

dataElements.label: considers (1)
dataElements.description: care process evaluations  considers any target conditions

dataElements.label: evaluates
dataElements.description: care process evaluations  evaluates any care processes

classes.label: care investigation
classes.description: care activity with the intention to clarify one or more health conditions of a subject of care

dataElements.label: clarifies
dataElements.description: one care investigation shall clarifies a health state

dataElements.label: reveals
dataElements.description: care investigations shall reveals health condition one or more health conditions

metadata.value: Care investigations add and improve information about aspects of a health state.

Some primary care investigations may have an intended or secondary therapeutic effect, while some activities can be both investigative and therapeutic in nature (e.g. fibroscopy, interventional cardiology).

classes.label: care mandate
classes.description: mandate based on a commitment and either an informed consent or an authorization by law defining the rights and obligations of one care actor with regard to their involvement in care processes performed for a specific subject of care

dataElements.label: concerns
dataElements.description: care mandates shall concerns a subject of care

dataElements.label: derives from
dataElements.description: any care mandates shall derives from any care mandates

dataElements.label: assigned to
dataElements.description: care mandates shall assigned to a care actor

dataElements.label: recorded in
dataElements.description: any care mandates  recorded in any health records

dataElements.label: implies access to
dataElements.description: care mandates shall implies access to one or more health records

dataElements.label: regulates
dataElements.description: any care mandates shall regulates one or more care processes

dataElements.label: requires (1)
dataElements.description: care mandates  requires any authorizations by law

dataElements.label: requires care commitment
dataElements.description: care mandates shall requires care commitment a care commitment

metadata.value: Relevant information related to care commitment mandates (including demands for care commitment informed consents, objections, care commitment commitments, etc.) is recorded in care commitment records where the information is made available for concerned care commitment actors as care commitment concerns.
Typically, a care mandate is assigned by one care actor to another.
A care mandate can be explicit or implicit.

classes.label: clinical care
classes.description: care activity concerned with physical or mental health needs

classes.label: initial contact
classes.description: care contact starting a continuity of care process

dataElements.label: establishes
dataElements.description: initial contacts shall establishes a continuity of care process

classes.label: request for service
classes.description: request for care where a care professional asks a care provider to deliver one or more care service offerings

metadata.value: A care provider may accept or decline a request for service.
The responsibility for the requested care provider activities is held by the performer but they will be performed under the care period mandate of the requester.
Request for a care assessment, an operation, a wheelchair.
Request for a second opinion where the responsibility remains with the requesting party.

classes.label: health condition assessment
classes.description: care activity where an assessment related to health conditions of the subject of care is made

classes.label: care information for import
classes.description: care information that is a candidate for import into a professional health record after a care professional has confirmed its clinical relevance to that professional health record

dataElements.label: imported into
dataElements.description: care information for import shall imported into a professional health record

metadata.value: Figure 147 —  care information for import (UML Representation)

classes.label: care commitment
classes.description: acceptance of a request for care including commitment by the care actor to perform requested care activities

dataElements.label: relates to the provision of
dataElements.description: care commitments  relates to the provision of any care activities

metadata.value: The care commitment is the promise by the care actor to perform care activities. This also means that the care provider accepts and confirms the pending care mandate issued through the proposed care plan.  It is only once the care commitment has been stated that an effective care mandate exists and will be the legal framework for all care activities of the subsequent care process.
Implicitly, a care commitment results from a dialogue with the subject of care or someone on behalf of the subject of care within a care needs assessment.

classes.label: care planning
classes.description: care activity management where a care plan is managed

dataElements.label: based upon
dataElements.description: any care planning  based upon any care guidelines

classes.label: clinical care plan
classes.description: care plan addressing clinical issues

classes.label: mandate
classes.description: commission to act

metadata.value: A request for care means that an individual asks for help by care provider. But it is combined with the authorisation for the care provider to access the necessary care resources including documentation and deliver those services requested by the individual. Such a request contains a mandate given to the care provider to provide care.

A mandate is assigned by someone or as the consequence of legislation. It is always an obligation to do something together with the associated authority to do that. If a mission is given to somebody, this actor needs authority to perform what is necessary to fulfil the mission.

classes.label: indirect care activity period
classes.description: care activity period without the involvement of the subject of care

metadata.value: Period taken for the planning of care.
The period where care activities are performed without the presence of the subject of care to decide whether a referral or a request for initial contact should be accepted or not.
Time spent working on laboratory or tissue specimens taken from a subject of care.
Figure 86 —  indirect care activity period (UML Representation)

classes.label: care service directory
classes.description: directory of the types of care service offerings offered by one or more care providers

classes.label: adverse event management
classes.description: set of care activities performed in response to an adverse event

classes.label: social need
classes.description: health need related to social determinants of health

classes.label: health related period
classes.description: time interval related to the health or care of a subject of care

dataElements.label: health related period
dataElements.description: one health related period  health related period any health related periods

dataElements.label: episode for
dataElements.description: a health related period shall episode for one or more continuity of care processes

metadata.value: A health related period is delineated by a “start date and time” and an “end date and time”. While the statement of the start date is generally easy, the identification of the end date may be subject to specific rules that have to be agreed upon locally.

Period of a head ache, period of pneumonia, period of diabetes mellitus from first onset and often until death, period of receiving or delivering psychotherapy, period of social authority surveillance of suspected abuse in a family, period where a care provider is mandated to provide care for a subject of care’s health issue, period of investigation and therapy of prostate cancer, period of delivering antibiotic therapy.
A health related period may be specialized in relation to a number of situations – a specific continuity of care process, care professional, a specific department, a specific health matter, etc.

classes.label: care performer
classes.description: role adopted by a person when performing care

dataElements.label: delivers
dataElements.description: care performers shall delivers one or more care activities

classes.label: health
classes.description: state of complete physical, mental and social well-being

metadata.value: The definition is drawn from the WHO description of health: a state of complete physical, mental and social well-being and not merely the absence of disease or infirmity.

classes.label: care service offering
classes.description: set of care activities offered by care provider

dataElements.label: listed in
dataElements.description: any care service shall listed in one or more care service directories

metadata.value: A process consists of activities. These can be bundled in groups called services.
A service can be an entire process or a part of a process. In the second case, the result of the service contributes to the outcome of the process.

classes.label: health report
classes.description: health record extract conveying specifically focused care information in order to fulfil current information needs of the recipient

classes.label: person
classes.description: individual human being

classes.label: care activity delay
classes.description: health related period during which a care activity is planned but not started

classes.label: health matter
classes.description: circumstance contributing to the health state of a person

dataElements.label: labels
dataElements.description: care matters shall labels a health record component

dataElements.label: health thread
dataElements.description: any care matters  health thread any health threads

metadata.value: This can be a matter which calls for care intervention, e.g. a medical statement, and immunisation, a treatment, but it can also be a matter which the person can manage alone, e.g. by means of self-care activities, or needs no management.

classes.label: care resource
classes.description: resource needed to perform care activities

metadata.value: As a care process develops, the care resources typically follow a life cycle. Examples of steps of such a life cycle are: “available”, “booked”, “provided”, “in use”, “consumed”, etc.

classes.label: social record extract
classes.description: part or all of a social record extracted for the purpose of communication

classes.label: working diagnosis
classes.description: professionally assessed condition that one or more care professionals have determined to be the most consistent with observed conditions

dataElements.label: consistent with
dataElements.description: working diagnoses  consistent with any observed conditions

metadata.value: A working diagnosis is often identified in the continuity of care process as a summary after the planned care investigations are completed. A working diagnosis in this stage is often called a diagnosis. An excluded condition could correspondingly be called a negation of a working diagnosis.

A working diagnosis is used as a label for the considered condition that one or more care professionals assess as the most probable health condition and that could be concluded after further observations. The basis for such assessments is the already observed conditions.
Different care professionals may make different interpretations and assessments of the observed conditions and thereby come to different conclusions and different working.

classes.label: commitment
