classes.description: obligation by one or more of the participants in an act to comply with a rule or perform a contract

metadata.value: The enterprise object(s) participating in an action of commitment may be parties or agents acting on behalf of a party or parties. In the case of an action of commitment by an agent, the principal becomes obligated.

classes.label: non-ratified care information
classes.description: care information the relevance of which has not been assessed and explicitly stated as valid by a care professional

dataElements.label: recorded in
dataElements.description: any non-ratified care information shall recorded in one or more health records

dataElements.label: becomes
dataElements.description: any non-ratified care information shall becomes one or more care information for import

metadata.value: Regardless of the technical process meant practically, care information cannot be “inserted” into a professional health record unless ratified by a care professional. Until a decision is made regarding their insertion or rejection, they are held in a secure temporary storage. An exception to this would be when a care professional would directly import care information from a shareable data repository into the local health record they hold. In the case where this import resulted from a request to the care actor in the custody of whom the shareable data repository is placed, that care information would then need validation.

It is possible that care information that has been received and is proposed for incorporation in a professional health record has not yet been reviewed for validity or context by the care professional and as such its accuracy can remain arguable. In this respect, clinical ratification differs both from attesting information for the needs of an audit trail, or from committing information in the perspective of sending out messages containing care information outlined in ISO 13606-1.

classes.label: informed consent
classes.description: permission to perform care activities, voluntarily given by a subject of care with consent competence or by a subject of care proxy with consent competence after having been informed about the purpose and the possible results of the care activities

dataElements.label: requires
dataElements.description: informed consents  requires any consent competences

metadata.value: A care mandate requires either informed consent or authorization by law.

classes.label: care appointment
classes.description: appointment for a contact

dataElements.label: scheduled in
dataElements.description: any care appointments  scheduled in any care plan

dataElements.label: appointment for
dataElements.description: any care appointments  appointment for a care contact

classes.label: considered condition
classes.description: potential health condition considered by a care actor based on one or more observed conditions

dataElements.label: based on
dataElements.description: considered conditions shall based on one or more observed conditions

metadata.value: A considered condition remains considered until the associated observed conditions are changed or completed. Care investigation or care treatment can result in new observations that can verify or not verify the considered condition. When a considered condition is verified, it becomes an observed condition or professionally assessed condition that may be a working diagnosis. If a considered condition is discounted, it is transformed into an excluded condition.

A referral within a continuity of care process is normally motivated by one or several observed conditions or professionally assessed conditions. However, the referral also normally includes a question that the care investigation is supposed to get an answer to. The question formulated as a potential condition is a considered condition.

A request for care normally includes a health condition or symptom observed by the subject of care and also a question about what the reason for that symptom might be. It is the potential health condition in this question (the health condition behind the symptom) that is called a considered condition.

classes.label: care activity bundle
classes.description: set of care activities

dataElements.label: includes
dataElements.description: any care activities bundle may include  care activities bundle.

metadata.value: A care activities bundle may be delineated using a health thread comprising care activities.
A care activities bundle can be offered as a care service.
All care activities provided to a subject of care in provision of, during and in the aftermath of a planned surgical operation, in relation to that operation.

classes.label: point of care
classes.description: location where direct care activities are performed

metadata.value: Activities referred are not limited to care provider activities; self-care activities are included.
Location refers to the geographical location of the subject of care, not the body area of the subject of care that the treatment is applied to.
Surgery room, ward, ambulance, roadside, home of the subject of care, school.

classes.label: role
classes.description: set of competencies or performances, or both, that are associated with a task

classes.label: health condition period
classes.description: health related period during which a health condition has been observed

metadata.value: Health condition period refers only to the observation of the health condition for example, the time interval during which a subject of care has observed a bleeding. The concept episode of care is referring to the care activities.

classes.label: information model
classes.description: formal model of a bounded set of facts, concepts or instructions to meet a specified requirement

classes.label: prescribed third party activity
classes.description: care third party activity prescribed by a care professional

metadata.value: Figure 59 —  prescribed third party activity (UML Representation)

classes.label: care activity management
classes.description: care activity during which the status of care activities in a care plan are changed

dataElements.label: manages
dataElements.description: care activities management shall manages one or more care activities

metadata.value: Example statuses for care activities are: planned, scheduled, resource allocated, ongoing, performed, evaluated.

classes.label: social issue
classes.description: health issue that is a social matter
